creator: empty.japan
caption:
I often get messages from people worried about not being welcome by neighbors in Japan, especially in the countryside. From my experience, if you respect the community—keeping noise down, sorting your trash, and keeping things clean—you’ll have no problems. Yes, Japan may feel like it has a lot of rules, but they’re part of what keeps everything so tidy and organized. Of course, this is just my perspective, and I understand there might be exceptions. Funny story—when I lived in Kyoto, I had a couple of BBQs on my terrace... and ended up with a note from management! 😅 At first, I was frustrated, but then I realized those rules were there for a reason, and they’ve really helped make life in the community smooth and enjoyable.

So, if you’re respectful and mindful of the local customs, there’s nothing to worry about.  In fact, you’ll probably end up with one of the most welcoming neighbor communities you’ve ever had, and they’ll be happy to help you out in any sort of trouble. The only challenge might be figuring out what to do with all the fruits and veggies your neighbors will generously give you 😂
